name: Pennsylvania Data Privacy Act
summary: Pennsylvania's Data Privacy Act establishes consumer data privacy rights for one of the largest US state economies. It follows the Virginia/Connecticut framework with rights to access, delete, and opt out of data sales, while imposing data protection assessment requirements and processor-controller contractual obligations.
keyRequirements: Consumer rights to access, correct, delete, and obtain a portable copy of personal data
Right to opt out of sale of personal data, targeted advertising, and profiling
Data controllers must provide clear and accessible privacy notices
Data protection assessments required for targeted advertising, sale of data, and sensitive data processing
Data processing agreements with mandatory security and confidentiality provisions
Heightened consent requirements for processing sensitive personal data
penalties: Enforcement exclusively by the Pennsylvania Attorney General with civil penalties up to $7,500 per violation. No private right of action. 30-day cure period for violations.
